mapping.a_element: diffusion process on small-world networks with distance-dependent random links
mapping.b_element: spread of infection on small-world networks

mapping.a_element: random walker or surface motion on the network
phase diagram with transient and recurrent phases in random walks
mapping.b_element: transition from fluctuating endemic state to self-sustained oscillations

mapping.a_element: annealed (fast link rearrangement) and quenched (slow link rearrangement) cases
mapping.b_element: network disorder parameter (from ordered lattices to random graphs)

mapping.a_element: breakdown of mean-field scaling in low-dimensional small-world networks
mapping.b_element: transition to self-sustained oscillations at finite disorder

mapping.a_element: network Laplacian and its eigenvalues governing diffusion
mapping.b_element: dynamics of infection spread governed by network structure

brief_justification: Both papers study dynamical processes (diffusion vs. infection spread) on small-world networks, where the network's disorder parameter drives a phase transition. The diffusion process's transient/recurrent phases and mean-field breakdown correspond to the epidemiological model's transition from endemic states to self-sustained oscillations, with the network Laplacian and disorder parameter playing analogous roles in governing the dynamics.
